focus_points.label: Late nights — temporary push or default?
focus_points.reason: Notes say Lin has worked late for about three weeks. Could be a short post-launch sprint, or a sign the workload and interrupt load are drifting into a default pattern — worth clarifying what’s driving the current pace now that the launches are done.

focus_points.label: What only Lin should own vs delegate
focus_points.reason: Whether they’ve been able to hand off real ownership after the launches, or are quietly carrying work that could be distributed. At Head of Product, this is the fastest way to spot whether autonomy and capacity are scaling with the role.

focus_points.label: What Lin is saying yes to now
focus_points.reason: Notes mention two major features delivered ahead of schedule, but the late work is continuing. One possibility is priorities shifted into new initiatives or follow-through; another is firefighting and rework. Worth clarifying what the next set of commitments actually are and what’s being deprioritized.

focus_points.label: What Lin would want more from you
focus_points.reason: Whether he needs more air cover, faster decisions from you, or help removing organizational friction so the late nights don’t become the mechanism for getting things done. At this seniority, asking directly for what support he wants is often what turns a pattern around.
